untranslatable_reason: This is a philosophy/filter statement about expectancy thresholds and win-rate ratios, not a position sizing rule. It describes entry conditions (expectancy ≥ 0.0R, win rate ≥ 35%, win/loss ratio ≥ 1.5) that qualify a trade for execution, but contains no directive on HOW to size positions (e.g., no % of capital, no R-multiple, no scaling method). The rule teaches *which trades to take*, not *how much to risk on them*.
